mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength and robust corporate revenue growth, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term as earnings estimates are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment is a primary driver of aggregate demand.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between monthly changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it triggers market expectations of imminent monetary policy tightening by the central bank, increasing the equity risk premium and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger condition is met, evaluated over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of slowing real GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a high probability of a forthcoming recession. This combined signal of weakening aggregate demand and labor market deterioration causes a sharp repricing of equity risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1% annualized AND UNRATE 3-month change > 0.3pp
failure_condition: The specified joint signal does not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with at least 60% accuracy in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, any increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a pivotal shift away from maximum accommodation. This regime change negatively impacts valuations for long-duration assets like equities, leading to negative short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS level was below 0.25% in the prior month and increased in the current month.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS value of 0.09 is within the ZIRP band.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a rate hike from ZIRP is not statistically significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors of the economy. This is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, leading to lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average crosses below its 12-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 60-day returns is below 50% over two consecutive full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) demonstrates strong consumer health and spending momentum. As consumer spending is a primary driver of corporate revenue, this signal leads to positive equity returns over very short horizons as investors react to the data.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a top-quartile monthly RSAFS release is not statistically different from the average return following a bottom-quartile release.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and contracting industrial production (INDPRO) signals a stagflationary environment. This is exceptionally damaging for equities, as it combines rising input costs that compress margins with falling demand that reduces revenues.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 4% AND INDPRO YoY change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Historical stagflationary periods (e.g., 1970s) were poor for equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return during periods when the trigger condition is met is positive over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) below the estimated natural rate indicates a tight labor market. While this can eventually lead to wage inflation and policy tightening, in the short term it is interpreted as a sign of a strong economy, boosting investor sentiment and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 5.2 is below the specified threshold.
failure_condition: The correlation between the UNRATE level and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) during periods when UNRATE is below 5.5%.
